name: Ruta de los Pescadores — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro
region_intro: Jogging routes around Arenas De San Pedro traverse a varied landscape, situated on the southern slopes of the Sierra de Gredos within the Tiétar Valley. The region features mountain foothills, the Arenal River with its natural pools, and extensive forests of oak, chestnut, and pine. This combination provides diverse terrain, from gentle riverbanks to trails with significant elevation changes, suitable for various running preferences. Best jogging routes around Arenas De San Pedro The most popular jogging route is Ruta de los Pescadores — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This route follows the Río Arenal, passing by natural pools and the historic Aquelcabos Medieval Bridge. Another top favourite among local runners is Arenas de San Pedro – Poza de Quirol loop from Arenas de San Pedro, a difficult 18.7 miles (30.0 km) path. This longer route offers extensive views across the Tiétar Valley and the Sierra de Gredos foothills. Local runners also love the Río Cuevas Reservoir – Aquelcabos Medieval Bridge loop from Arenas de San Pedro, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Río Cuevas Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Jogging around Arenas De San Pedro is defined by the rugged Sierra de Gredos foothills, the serene Arenal River with its natural pools, and the expansive Tiétar Valley. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging running experiences. The routes in Arenas De San Pedro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 160 runners have used komoot to explore Arenas De San Pedro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Arenas de San Pedro?
A: Arenas de San Pedro offers a wide selection of running routes. There are over 20 routes available, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Arenas de San Pedro?
A: The region provides diverse terrain for jogging. You'll find paths along the serene Arenal River, through lush oak, chestnut, and pine forests in the Tiétar Valley, and trails with varying elevations in the foothills of the rugged Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Arenas de San Pedro?
A: Yes, there are several moderate routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge – Aquelcabos Medieval Bridge loop from Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5 km path that offers a pleasant experience.

Q: Are there challenging running trails for experienced runners in the area?
A: Absolutely. Arenas de San Pedro features 10 difficult running routes for experienced runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquelcabos Medieval Bridge – Cruz de la Tendera loop from Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 12.2 km route with significant elevation changes, offering a good test of endurance.

Q: Are there jogging routes that pass by natural pools or rivers?
A: Many routes in Arenas de San Pedro follow the Arenal River, leading past beautiful natural pools and gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1157709508/ruta-de-los-pescadores-circular-desde-arenas-de-san-pedro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Pescadores — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering scenic views of the river and its transparent waters.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in Arenas de San Pedro?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer distances, the region has options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31874533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenas de San Pedro – Poza de Quirol loop from Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30 km path that provides extensive views across the Tiétar Valley and the Sierra de Gredos foothills.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Arenas de San Pedro?
A: Jogging routes often feature stunning natural beauty and historical landmarks. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5678370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquelcabos Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2840991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro de Alcántara Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2544560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Pedro Bernardo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Arenas de San Pedro are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cuevas Reservoir – Aquelcabos Medieval Bridge loop from Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Arenas de San Pedro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the rugged Sierra de Gredos foothills to the serene Arenal River, and the well-maintained trails that offer both moderate and challenging running experiences.

Q: Are there running trails with good viewpoints in the region?
A: Many trails offer spectacular views, especially those in the higher elevations or along the valley. Routes traversing the lower slopes of the Sierra de Gredos often provide incredible vistas of the Tiétar Valley and the surrounding natural beauty, such as the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31874533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenas de San Pedro – Poza de Quirol loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Arenas de San Pedro?
A: The region's climate makes it enjoyable for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Even in summer, routes along the Arenal River provide shaded paths and the refreshing natural pools are a welcome sight.
tips:
In addition to its extensive heritage, Arenas de San Pedro is a tourist attraction for its famous Eagle Caves, discovered in 1963 and located six kilometers from the town.

In its town center, highlights include the Castle of the Triste Condesa, with its large Keep; the 16th-century Gothic Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, with its splendid bell tower; and the palace of Infante Don Luis de Borbón. Also a must-see is the 16th-century Sanctuary of San Pedro de Alcántara, declared a Historic-Artistic Monument in 1972. Located three kilometers from Arenas, it was renovated in the 18th century following the directives of architect Ventura Rodríguez, who built a magnificent funerary chapel. A thornless rosebush grows in the sanctuary's garden, which, according to legend, is a miraculous work of Saint Peter of Alcántara. The Royal Chapel Museum displays interesting collections of liturgical goldsmithing.

The trail runs along a charming path between the pines to the Old Bridge of the Pelayos River. The pass is ancient, the stones of the bridge are covered with vines and vegetation everywhere in a magical and seductive environment populated by giant holly trees. The road is close, with a recreational area and fountain. The trail crosses the bridge and continues along the cobblestone path.
Path that runs along the side of the Río Arenal itself, showing its clear waters coming from the mountains of the Central System. Beautiful path between riparian forests!

The path goes up a small slope and the detour appears that goes down to the footbridge of Poza de Quirol on the left. The trail goes down some steep steps to the canyon where the pedestrian crossing is, in the Carquesas de los Pereñiles area, a forest of durillos, strawberry trees, heathers and a leafy little forest of parrots, a botanical exclusivity of the valley that gives an exuberance forest with tunnels in the dense trees and passageways that are authentic jungles of Mediterranean scrub.
